folio.label: 12r
folio.l: And spak of mirthe , a mong´ other thynges
Whan æ he had mad , our rekenynges
And seide , lo lordyngges trewely
ñe ben to me , rigút welcome hertily
For be my treuth , if that I shal nat lye
I sey nat æis ©ere , so merie a companye
At ones , in this herberwe , as is nowe
Fayn wold I do ©ow myrthe , & I wist howe
And of a mirthe , I am ryght now be æou©t´
To don ©ow ease , and it shal cost nou©t´
¢ ñe gon to Caunterbury , god ©ow spede
That blisful martir , quyte ©ow ©oure mede
And wel I wot , as ©e gon by the weye
ñe shapen ©ow to talken , and to pleye
For trewely , comfort ne mirthe is non
To ride by the weye , as dom as it were a ston
And æäfore wyl I , maken ©ow disport ´
As I seide erst , and do ©ow som comfort´
And if ©ow like , alle be one assent
To stonden , at my Iuggement´
And for to werken , as I shal ©ow sey
To morwe , whan ©e riden by the wey
Now be my faders soule , æat is dede
But ©e be merie , I wyl ©eue ©ow myn hede
Holde vp ©oure hondes , w oute more speche
Oure conseil was nou©t , longe for to seche

note.notetxt: 484the first half of the line is in a lighter ink. This ink is used in line 785 and in half line 786 until w . Probably an effect of the parchment.ODR

folio.l: Vs æou©t it was nat worthy , to make it nyce
And gunted hi~ , w oute more a vyse
And bad hi~ sey , his verdyt as hi~ lest
¢ Lordynggã quod he , now herkeneth for the best´
But take it nougút , I prey ©ow in disdeyn
This is æe poynt , to speke it short & pleyn
That eche of ©ow , to short with ©oure weye
In this viage , shal telle tales tweye
To Caunterburyward , I mene it so
And homward , he shal telle othere two
Of auentures , that whilom , han byfalle
And which of ©ow , bereth hi~ best of alle
That is to seyn , that telleth in æis cas
Tales , of most sentence and solas
Shal haue a soper , at oure alder cost´
